findings.message: get_stock() now sends a new query parameter (?include_location=true) on the contracted GET /inventory/{id} interaction, but the consumer pact in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py declares the request as `.with_request("GET", "/inventory/1")` with no query parameter. The committed/published pact therefore won't reflect the consumer's actual request, so provider verification will replay the old query-less request and never confirm the provider honors `include_location`. (Pact V4 query matching may also reject the unexpected param, failing the consumer test.) The reading of `location` itself is additive/optional (`.get("location")`) and not a concern.
findings.suggestion: Update the consumer pact interaction to declare the query parameter (e.g. add `.with_query_parameter("include_location", "true")` to the `with_request(...)` builder), then regenerate and republish pacts/demo-inventory.json so the provider is verified against the new request shape.
